sampleDeliberations.opportunity: Will Jesse Jackson Jr. be the Democratic Nominee for IL-02?
sampleDeliberations.reasoning: Extreme illiquidity on both sides. Sub-$1000 volume. Spread exists because nobody is trading, not because of information asymmetry.

technician.reason: No meaningful order book depth on either platform

detective.reason: Resolution criteria may differ between platforms

devil.reason: Liquidity trap — no exit path even if correct

arbiter.reason: Unanimous pass. Gap is artifact of thin markets.

sampleDeliberations.opportunity: Will the upper bound of the target federal funds rate be 3.25% at the end of 2026?
sampleDeliberations.reasoning: Structural contract differences. Polymarket resolves on Fed announcement, Kalshi resolves on end-of-year rate. Different questions despite similar wording.

devil.reason: Even with identical criteria, 15% spread on macro markets is noise

sampleDeliberations.reasoning: Large spread but Kalshi market has near-zero liquidity. Polymarket price is more reliable given 10x volume. Executing on Kalshi side would move the market entirely.

technician.reason: Kalshi book is empty — any buy would be 100% of volume

sentinel.reason: No directional catalyst to justify entry

detective.reason: Kalshi price is stale, not mispriced

devil.reason: Spread is wide because Kalshi side is abandoned

sampleDeliberations.opportunity: Will Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva win the 2026 Brazilian presidential election?
sampleDeliberations.reasoning: 6.6% spread is within normal noise for long-dated political markets. Both platforms show reasonable volume. No information edge.

technician.reason: Spread within historical noise band for political markets

sentinel.reason: No breaking news or polling shift

detective.reason: Resolution criteria aligned — genuine match, just no edge

devil.reason: 6.6% is not enough to cover execution risk on political markets

arbiter.reason: Legitimate match, no exploitable gap. This is markets agreeing.

securityIncidents.description: AI agent committed .env file with wallet private key to public GitHub repository
securityIncidents.impact: $700 drained from wallet in 15 minutes by automated scanning bot
securityIncidents.resolution: Repository deleted, credentials rotated, wallet abandoned
securityIncidents.rootCause: Subagent optimized for task completion over security; no pre-commit secret scanning

securityIncidents.description: AI agent hardcoded OpenRouter API key as fallback default in TypeScript file
securityIncidents.impact: Key exposed in public repo, caught by human before bot exploitation
securityIncidents.resolution: Key removed from code, rotated at provider
securityIncidents.rootCause: Subagent treated 'private repo' as secure; credentials passed in task description

securityIncidents.description: AI agent hardcoded wallet private key in deploy script and pushed to public repo
securityIncidents.impact: $12 drained from wallet in 3 minutes by automated scanning bot
securityIncidents.resolution: Repository deleted and recreated clean, wallet abandoned
securityIncidents.rootCause: Subagent inlined private key for 'convenience'; no diff review before push

securityFixes: PR-only workflow — no direct pushes to main branch
GitHub Secret Scanning + Push Protection enabled
Credential isolation — subagent task descriptions reference env var names only, never actual secrets
Pre-commit secret scanning via grep pattern matching
Rules permanently encoded in agent memory (AGENTS.md, MEMORY.md)
